text.title: MCP Basics: Model Context Protocol Server Development Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding the mcp-basics methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: First MCP server MUST start with exactly one tool. Adding more tools is deferred until the first tool ships + has 1 week of usage.
rule.rationale: Shipping one tool first forces the team to learn the SDK + transport + schema + install flow; adding tools is much easier afterward.

rule.statement: First-server transport MUST be stdio. streamable-http requires an additional deploy story; defer until at least one stdio server is live.
rule.rationale: stdio works locally with `claude mcp add ... -- npx -y ...`; minimum viable path.

rule.statement: The single tool MUST declare a Zod (TS) or Pydantic (Python) schema. Schema-less tools defeat the value of MCP and become tech debt.
rule.rationale: Even the first tool needs schema; without it the model invents arguments and the integration fails on day one.

rule.statement: README MUST include the exact `claude mcp add ...` install command with env-var placeholders. Discovery by humans depends on this.
rule.rationale: Users abandon servers whose install command is buried; install command in README is the discoverability minimum.

rule.statement: Before pushing to the team, MUST run `mcp-inspector` against the server and confirm the tool appears + schema validates + a sample call returns a valid response.
rule.rationale: Inspector is the SDK-blessed smoke test; running it catches transport / schema / registration errors before users see them.
